title: A Prayer for the Beginning of Lent
text:
Lord our God,
you invite us to turn away from sin and to fix our hearts on you.

In this holy season of Lent, grant us the grace of genuine repentance — not merely the outward gesture of fasting, but the inward transformation of a contrite heart.

May we use these forty days wisely: to pray with greater fervour, to fast with discipline and love, and to give generously to those who lack what we take for granted.

Draw us closer to the Cross, that in dying to ourselves we may rise with your Son, Jesus Christ, who lives and reigns with you and the Holy Spirit, one God, for ever and ever.

Amen.
scripture: Return to me with your whole heart, with fasting, weeping, and mourning. Rend your hearts, not your garments. — Joel 2:12–13
reflection: What is one thing I can let go of this Lent to make more room for God?
source: Ash Wednesday Collect, Roman Missal (3rd Edition)

title: For Those Who Suffer
text:
Gracious God, during this season of Lent we bring before you all who carry heavy burdens — those living with illness, those fleeing violence, those who hunger while we are fed.

Forgive us for the times we have looked away. Open our eyes to the face of Christ in the suffering stranger.

May our fasting make us hungry enough to notice those who cannot choose when they eat. May our giving be more than what we can easily spare. May our prayer be not only words, but a changed life.

We ask this through Jesus Christ, who took on our poverty that we might share in his richness.

Amen.
scripture: Whatever you did for one of the least of these brothers and sisters of mine, you did for me. — Matthew 25:40
reflection: Who in my life is carrying a burden I have not yet noticed?
attribution: Inspired by CAFOD Lenten Reflection Series

title: Lord, Make Me an Instrument
scripture: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good. — Romans 12:21
reflection: Where is God calling me to be a peacemaker today?
